pr.title: [scripts] Add diagnostic script for GitHub connector showing 'Connected' but no tools (#61682)
pr.body:
## Summary

Adds a PowerShell diagnostic/repair script for Windows users affected by the recurring bug where the GitHub MCP connector in Cowork shows **'Connected'** status but exposes **zero tools**.

Closes #61682

## Root Cause Analysis

Based on the chain of related issues (#28695, #41658, #57589), the root cause is the OAuth token missing the \user:mcp_servers\ scope. The connector's SSE transport connects successfully (showing 'Connected'), but the \	ools/list\ MCP RPC fails because the token doesn't have the required scope. The error is swallowed silently, resulting in 0 tools registered.

This has regressed multiple times after being 'fixed' in #23775.

## What This Script Does

The \Diagnose-GitHubConnector.ps1\ script checks the Claude Desktop installation, enumerates Windows Credential Manager for OAuth tokens, inspects MCP connector cache, can clear stale cache (\-Force\), and links to all relevant issues.

## Usage

\\\powershell
.\scripts\Diagnose-GitHubConnector.ps1 -CheckOnly
.\scripts\Diagnose-GitHubConnector.ps1 -Force
\\\

## Note

This is a diagnostic/workaround tool. The permanent fix needs to be in the desktop app OAuth flow to request \user:mcp_servers\ scope reliably.

cc #23775, #28695, #41658, #57589
pr.author: giruuuuj
